root_text.an6.117:1.1: “Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo kāye kāyānupassī viharituṁ.
root_text.an6.117:1.3: Kammārāmataṁ, bhassārāmataṁ, niddārāmataṁ, saṅgaṇikārāmataṁ, indriyesu aguttadvārataṁ, bhojane amattaññutaṁ.
root_text.an6.117:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme appahāya abhabbo kāye kāyānupassī viharituṁ.
root_text.an6.117:2.1: Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo kāye kāyānupassī viharituṁ.
root_text.an6.117:2.3: Kammārāmataṁ, bhassārāmataṁ, niddārāmataṁ, saṅgaṇikārāmataṁ, indriyesu aguttadvārataṁ, bhojane amattaññutaṁ.
root_text.an6.117:2.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme pahāya bhabbo kāye kāyānupassī viharitun”ti.

translation_text.an6.117:1.1: “Mendicants, without giving up these six qualities you can’t meditate observing an aspect of the body.
translation_text.an6.117:1.3: Relishing work, talk, sleep, and company, not guarding the sense doors, and eating too much.
translation_text.an6.117:1.4: Without giving up these six qualities you can’t meditate observing an aspect of the body.
translation_text.an6.117:2.1: But after giving up these six qualities you can meditate observing an aspect of the body.
translation_text.an6.117:2.3: Relishing work, talk, sleep, and company, not guarding the sense doors, and eating too much.
translation_text.an6.117:2.4: After giving up these six qualities you can meditate observing an aspect of the body.”
